prevalence.value: 1-5% (higher in vegetarians, older adults, and low-income groups)
prevalence.trend: stable; high background B12 deficiency in some groups

vulnerablePopulations: low-income groups with poor diet diversity
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Macrocytosis with low B12/folate; clinical features include glossitis and neuropathy; MMA testing limited

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; depends on adherence and addressing dietary causes

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: CBC available; B12 testing varies; folate supplementation common in pregnancy programs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: B12 replacement and folate supplementation when indicated; dietary counseling and evaluation for malabsorption
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High underlying B12 deficiency burden and limited lab confirmation in some settings
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost supplementation and widespread clinical recognition of nutritional anemia
